wheelStrategy.vixNote: VIX at 16.89 — BELOW 20 threshold. Wait for VIX > 20 before entering new CSP positions.
wheelStrategy.bollingerEntry: Daily chart, 20-period 2σ Bollinger Bands. Lower BB estimated ~$82–86 at current volatility. Wait for price to tag or breach lower band before selling CSP. Current price $89.94 is mid-to-upper band; no entry signal yet.

csp.exitRule: Close at 50% premium captured OR close 5 days before next earnings report. Do not hold through earnings.
csp.note: Moderate IV (~30–40% for large-cap SaaS). 1–2 contracts manageable ($7,800–$8,400 capital at risk per contract). If assigned, cost basis $78–84 is ~7–13% below current price — acceptable assignment level.

coveredCall.exitRule: Close at 50% premium captured. Re-sell if stock stays flat. Close before earnings.
coveredCall.note: If assigned on CSP (cost basis ~$78–84), sell CC at $92–96 to collect premium above assignment price while waiting for stock recovery.

exitRules.entryTrigger: Enter on any dip toward $86–88 (lower Bollinger Band entry). Current $91.18 is mid-band. IV at 76% is elevated — spreads preferred to reduce cost.
exitRules.profitExit: Close at 60–80% gain on premium. Target $120–125 for first exit (~30–37% upside), ride second half toward $145–155 for larger gain.
exitRules.timeStop: If stock has not reached $97+ by ~June 25 (40% of time elapsed), close. High IV makes time decay punishing.
exitRules.lossStop: Close at 40–50% loss on premium. IV crush post-earnings is the key risk if held too long.
exitRules.earningsHardStop: Close by July 24 (5 days before July 29 Q2 earnings) — no exceptions.

options.bestFor: Too short — 22 days left at the mandatory July 24 close makes this untenable. You exit with mostly extrinsic decay rather than capturing the move.
Best balance — 57 days left at exit, time value intact. High IV (76%) means consider a spread ($95/$120) to cut debit by 40–50%. Sep 19 aligns with the original Oct 2026 intent at lower cost.
Maximum time buffer but expensive in a high-IV environment. Appropriate only if expecting a multi-quarter re-rating rather than a single-catalyst move.

recommendation.title: Single-Leg Call — Slightly OTM
recommendation.entryNote: Enter AFTER May 5 — analyst day May 4 will cause IV crush. IV currently at 59 (52-week range 27–76).

companyOverview: Enterprise cloud software platform for IT, HR, and operations automation. Strong AI-driven growth via Now Platform. Q1 2026: revenue +19% YoY to $3.77B, subscription +22%. Analyst Day May 4 is a near-term catalyst.
catalysts: Q1 2026 beat on revenue and subscription growth
Analyst Day May 4 — potential positive re-rating
risks: IV crush risk around May 4 Analyst Day — enter after May 5
Stock only down ~9% from pre-earnings, not the full -15% threshold
High capex overhang narrative from broader tech sector
Breakeven at $106–107 requires meaningful stock move
dilution: No significant dilution risk. Standard corporate structure. Main risk is AI capex narrative weighing on sentiment.
